**Stefani Joanne Angelina Germanotta**[[a]](#cite_note-1) (born March 28, 1986), [known professionally](/wiki/Stage_name "Stage name") as **Lady Gaga**, is an American singer, songwriter, and actress. Known for her image reinventions and versatility across the entertainment industry, she is an influential figure in [popular music](/wiki/Popular_music "Popular music"). With estimated sales of 124 million records, she is one of the [best-selling music artists of all time](/wiki/List_of_best-selling_music_artists "List of best-selling music artists"). Publications such as *[Billboard](/wiki/Billboard_(magazine) "Billboard (magazine)")* and *[Rolling Stone](/wiki/Rolling_Stone "Rolling Stone")* have ranked her among the greatest artists in history.

After signing with [Interscope Records](/wiki/Interscope_Records "Interscope Records") in 2007, Gaga achieved global recognition with her debut album, *[The Fame](/wiki/The_Fame "The Fame")* (2008), and its reissue, *[The Fame Monster](/wiki/The_Fame_Monster "The Fame Monster")* (2009). The project yielded a string of successful singles, including "[Just Dance](/wiki/Just_Dance_(song) "Just Dance (song)")", "[Poker Face](/wiki/Poker_Face_(song) "Poker Face (song)")", and "[Bad Romance](/wiki/Bad_Romance "Bad Romance")", which made her one of the few artists with at least three Diamond-certified songs in the US. Her second studio album, *[Born This Way](/wiki/Born_This_Way_(album) "Born This Way (album)")* (2011), explored [electronic rock](/wiki/Electronic_rock "Electronic rock") and [techno-pop](/wiki/Techno-pop "Techno-pop") and sold [1.1 million copies first-week in the US](/wiki/Lists_of_fastest-selling_albums "Lists of fastest-selling albums"). Its [title track](/wiki/Born_This_Way_(song) "Born This Way (song)") became the fastest-selling song on the [iTunes Store](/wiki/ITunes_Store "ITunes Store"), with over one million downloads in less than a week. Following her [electronic dance music](/wiki/Electronic_dance_music "Electronic dance music")-influenced third album, *[Artpop](/wiki/Artpop "Artpop")* (2013), she pursued [jazz](/wiki/Jazz "Jazz") on the album *[Cheek to Cheek](/wiki/Cheek_to_Cheek_(album) "Cheek to Cheek (album)")* (2014) with [Tony Bennett](/wiki/Tony_Bennett "Tony Bennett"), and delved into [soft rock](/wiki/Soft_rock "Soft rock") on the album *[Joanne](/wiki/Joanne_(album) "Joanne (album)")* (2016).

Gaga also ventured into acting, gaining praise for her leading roles in the miniseries *[American Horror Story: Hotel](/wiki/American_Horror_Story:_Hotel "American Horror Story: Hotel")* (2015–2016) and the films *[A Star Is Born](/wiki/A_Star_Is_Born_(2018_film) "A Star Is Born (2018 film)")* (2018) and *[House of Gucci](/wiki/House_of_Gucci "House of Gucci")* (2021). Her contributions to the [*A Star Is Born* soundtrack](/wiki/A_Star_Is_Born_(2018_soundtrack) "A Star Is Born (2018 soundtrack)"), which spawned the chart-topping single "[Shallow](/wiki/Shallow_(Lady_Gaga_and_Bradley_Cooper_song) "Shallow (Lady Gaga and Bradley Cooper song)")", made her the first person to win an [Academy](/wiki/Academy_Awards "Academy Awards"), [BAFTA](/wiki/British_Academy_Film_Awards "British Academy Film Awards"), [Golden Globe](/wiki/Golden_Globe_Awards "Golden Globe Awards"), and [Grammy Award](/wiki/Grammy_Awards "Grammy Awards") in one year. Gaga returned to her early sound with the pop-oriented albums *[Chromatica](/wiki/Chromatica "Chromatica")* (2020) and *[Mayhem](/wiki/Mayhem_(Lady_Gaga_album) "Mayhem (Lady Gaga album)")* (2025), which respectively included the number-one singles "[Rain on Me](/wiki/Rain_on_Me_(Lady_Gaga_and_Ariana_Grande_song) "Rain on Me (Lady Gaga and Ariana Grande song)")" and "[Die with a Smile](/wiki/Die_with_a_Smile "Die with a Smile")". She also continued to explore jazz with *[Love for Sale](/wiki/Love_for_Sale_(Tony_Bennett_and_Lady_Gaga_album) "Love for Sale (Tony Bennett and Lady Gaga album)")* (2021), her second and final album with Bennett, and the soundtrack *[Harlequin](/wiki/Harlequin_(Lady_Gaga_album) "Harlequin (Lady Gaga album)")* (2024).

Gaga has seven number-one albums and six number-one songs on the US [*Billboard* 200](/wiki/Billboard_200 "Billboard 200") and [Hot 100](/wiki/Billboard_Hot_100 "Billboard Hot 100") charts, respectively. She is the only female artist with four singles that each sold [at least 10 million copies globally](/wiki/List_of_best-selling_singles "List of best-selling singles") and holds the record for the [most-attended concert by a woman](/wiki/List_of_most-attended_concerts "List of most-attended concerts"). The [highest-paid female musician](/wiki/Forbes_list_of_the_world%27s_highest-paid_musicians#Female "Forbes list of the world's highest-paid musicians") in 2011, [she has received](/wiki/List_of_awards_and_nominations_received_by_Lady_Gaga "List of awards and nominations received by Lady Gaga") 16 Grammy Awards, 22 [MTV Video Music Awards](/wiki/MTV_Video_Music_Awards "MTV Video Music Awards"), 2 Golden Globe Awards, a [Sports Emmy Award](/wiki/Sports_Emmy_Award "Sports Emmy Award"), and a recognition from the [Songwriters Hall of Fame](/wiki/Songwriters_Hall_of_Fame "Songwriters Hall of Fame"). Gaga's philanthropy and activism focus on mental health awareness and [LGBTQ rights](/wiki/LGBTQ_rights_by_country_or_territory "LGBTQ rights by country or territory"). Her business ventures include [vegan](/wiki/Veganism "Veganism") cosmetics brand [Haus Labs](/wiki/Haus_Labs "Haus Labs") and the non-profit [Born This Way Foundation](/wiki/Born_This_Way_Foundation "Born This Way Foundation"), which supports the wellness of young people.

## Life and career

### 1986–2004: Early life

Stefani Joanne Angelina Germanotta was born on March 28, 1986, at [Lenox Hill Hospital](/wiki/Lenox_Hill_Hospital "Lenox Hill Hospital") in the [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City") borough of [Manhattan](/wiki/Manhattan "Manhattan").[[1]](#cite_note-2) Her parents are [Cynthia Louise](/wiki/Cynthia_Germanotta "Cynthia Germanotta") ([née](/wiki/Birth_name#Maiden_and_married_names "Birth name") Bissett), a [philanthropist](/wiki/Philanthropist "Philanthropist") and business executive, and Joseph Germanotta, an Internet entrepreneur; both have [Italian](/wiki/Italian_Americans "Italian Americans") ancestry. Gaga said that her parents came from lower-class families and worked hard for everything.[[2]](#cite_note-3)[[3]](#cite_note-TimesOnlineDec09-4) They raised Gaga and her younger sister Natali in an [upper-middle-class](/wiki/Upper_middle_class_in_the_United_States "Upper middle class in the United States") Catholic family in the [Upper West Side](/wiki/Upper_West_Side "Upper West Side") neighborhood of New York.[[4]](#cite_note-5)[[5]](#cite_note-6)[[6]](#cite_note-7) From age 11, Gaga attended the [Convent of the Sacred Heart](/wiki/Convent_of_the_Sacred_Heart_(New_York_City) "Convent of the Sacred Heart (New York City)"), a private all-girls [Catholic school](/wiki/Catholic_school "Catholic school").[[7]](#cite_note-independent-8) Gaga has described her high-school self as "very dedicated, very studious, very disciplined" but also "a bit insecure". She considered herself a [misfit](/wiki/Outcast_(person) "Outcast (person)") and was mocked for "being either too provocative or too eccentric".[[8]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTETracy2013202-9)

Gaga began playing the piano at age four when her mother insisted she become "a cultured young woman". She took piano lessons and practiced through her childhood. The lessons taught Gaga to learn music by ear, which she preferred over reading sheet music. Gaga's parents encouraged her to pursue music and enrolled her in Creative Arts Camp.[[9]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEJohnson201220-10) As a teenager, she played at [open mic](/wiki/Open_mic "Open mic") nights.[[10]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEJohnson201226-11) Gaga played the lead roles of Adelaide in the play *[Guys and Dolls](/wiki/Guys_and_Dolls "Guys and Dolls")* and Philia in the play *[A Funny Thing Happened on the Way to the Forum](/wiki/A_Funny_Thing_Happened_on_the_Way_to_the_Forum "A Funny Thing Happened on the Way to the Forum")* at [Regis High School](/wiki/Regis_High_School_(New_York_City) "Regis High School (New York City)").[[11]](#cite_note-growingupgaga-12) She also studied [method acting](/wiki/Method_acting "Method acting") at the [Lee Strasberg Theatre and Film Institute](/wiki/Lee_Strasberg_Theatre_and_Film_Institute "Lee Strasberg Theatre and Film Institute") for ten years.[[12]](#cite_note-leestrasburgalumna-13) Her screen debut was a background appearance in the 2000 music video for [AC/DC](/wiki/AC/DC "AC/DC")'s song "[Stiff Upper Lip](/wiki/Stiff_Upper_Lip_(AC/DC_song) "Stiff Upper Lip (AC/DC song)")".[[13]](#cite_note-14) Gaga auditioned unsuccessfully for New York shows, though did appear in a small background role as a high-school student in a 2001 episode of *[The Sopranos](/wiki/The_Sopranos "The Sopranos")* titled "[The Telltale Moozadell](/wiki/The_Telltale_Moozadell "The Telltale Moozadell")".[[14]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEMorgan201027-15) Fan interest in that episode increased when a clip of Gaga's scene surfaced online in 2010.[[15]](#cite_note-16)[[16]](#cite_note-17) She later said of her inclination towards music:

> I don't know exactly where my affinity for music comes from, but it is the thing that comes easiest to me. When I was like three years old, I may have been even younger, my mom always tells this really embarrassing story of me propping myself up and playing the keys like this because I was too young and short to get all the way up there. Just go like this on the low end of the piano ... I was really, really good at piano, so my first instincts were to work so hard at practicing piano, and I might not have been a natural dancer, but I am a natural musician. That is the thing that I believe I am the greatest at.[[17]](#cite_note-18)

In 2003, Gaga gained early admission to [Collaborative Arts Project 21](/wiki/Collaborative_Arts_Project_21 "Collaborative Arts Project 21"), a music school at [New York University](/wiki/New_York_University "New York University") (NYU)'s [Tisch School of the Arts](/wiki/Tisch_School_of_the_Arts "Tisch School of the Arts"), and lived in an [NYU dorm](/wiki/New_York_University_residence_halls "New York University residence halls"). She studied music there and improved her songwriting skills by writing essays on art, religion, social issues, and politics, including a thesis on pop artists [Spencer Tunick](/wiki/Spencer_Tunick "Spencer Tunick") and [Damien Hirst](/wiki/Damien_Hirst "Damien Hirst").[[18]](#cite_note-19)[[19]](#cite_note-20) In 2005, Gaga withdrew from school during the second semester of her second year to focus on her music career.[[20]](#cite_note-mtv_55-21) That year, she also played an unsuspecting diner customer for [MTV](/wiki/MTV "MTV")'s *[Boiling Points](/wiki/Boiling_Points "Boiling Points")*, a prank reality television show.[[21]](#cite_note-22)

In a 2014 interview, Gaga discussed being raped at age 19 by her producer, and later undergoing mental and physical therapy for this.[[22]](#cite_note-23) She has [post-traumatic stress disorder](/wiki/Post-traumatic_stress_disorder "Post-traumatic stress disorder") (PTSD) and attributes it to the incident, stating that the trauma had changed her as a person, and would never leave her.[[23]](#cite_note-24) She has credited support from doctors, family, and friends with helping her.[[24]](#cite_note-25) Gaga later gave additional details about the rape, including that "the person who raped me [dropped me off pregnant](/wiki/Pregnancy_from_rape "Pregnancy from rape") on a corner at my parents' house because I was vomiting and sick. Because I'd been being abused. I was locked away in a studio for months."[[25]](#cite_note-26)

### 2005–2007: Career beginnings

In 2005, Gaga recorded two songs with rapper [Melle Mel](/wiki/Melle_Mel "Melle Mel") for an [audio book](/wiki/Audio_book "Audio book") accompanying Cricket Casey's children's novel *The Portal in the Park*.[[26]](#cite_note-27) She also formed a band called the SGBand with some friends from NYU.[[11]](#cite_note-growingupgaga-12)[[27]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEMorgan201031-28) They played gigs around New York and became a fixture of the downtown [Lower East Side](/wiki/Lower_East_Side "Lower East Side") club scene.[[11]](#cite_note-growingupgaga-12) After the 2006 [Songwriters Hall of Fame](/wiki/Songwriters_Hall_of_Fame "Songwriters Hall of Fame") New Songwriters Showcase at [the Cutting Room](/wiki/The_Cutting_Room "The Cutting Room") in June, talent scout Wendy Starland recommended her to music producer [Rob Fusari](/wiki/Rob_Fusari "Rob Fusari").[[28]](#cite_note-Fusari1-29) Fusari collaborated with Gaga, who traveled daily to New Jersey, helping to develop her songs and compose new material.[[29]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEMorgan201036-30) The producer said they began dating in May 2006, and claimed to have been the first person to call her "Lady Gaga", which was derived from [Queen](/wiki/Queen_(band) "Queen (band)")'s song "[Radio Ga Ga](/wiki/Radio_Ga_Ga "Radio Ga Ga")".[[30]](#cite_note-Fusari2-31) According to his account, the name was coined when on one occasion he attempted to call her "Radio Ga Ga" via text message, but the [spell checker](/wiki/Spell_checker "Spell checker") [autocorrected](/wiki/Autocorrect "Autocorrect") "Radio" to "Lady".[[31]](#cite_note-32) Their relationship lasted until January 2007.[[32]](#cite_note-rsannual-33)

[![A scantily-clad Gaga singing on a stage. She has a microphone and black stockings.](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/82/Lady_Gaga_at_Lollapalooza_2007_-_cropped.jpg/250px-Lady_Gaga_at_Lollapalooza_2007_-_cropped.jpg)](/wiki/File:Lady_Gaga_at_Lollapalooza_2007_-_cropped.jpg)

Gaga performing at [Lollapalooza](/wiki/Lollapalooza "Lollapalooza") in 2007

Fusari and Gaga established a company called Team Lovechild, LLC to promote her career.[[30]](#cite_note-Fusari2-31) They recorded and produced [electropop](/wiki/Electropop "Electropop") tracks, sending them to music industry executives. Joshua Sarubin, the head of [Artists and repertoire](/wiki/Artists_and_repertoire "Artists and repertoire") (A&R) at [Def Jam Recordings](/wiki/Def_Jam_Recordings "Def Jam Recordings"), responded positively and, after approval from Sarubin's boss [Antonio "L.A." Reid](/wiki/L.A._Reid "L.A. Reid"), Gaga was signed to Def Jam in September 2006.[[33]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEMorgan201045-34) She was dropped from the label three months later[[34]](#cite_note-Allmusic_bio-35) and returned to her family home for Christmas. Gaga began performing at [neo-burlesque](/wiki/Neo-burlesque "Neo-burlesque") shows, and said these represented freedom to her.[[35]](#cite_note-always-famous-36) During this time, she met performance artist [Lady Starlight](/wiki/Lady_Starlight "Lady Starlight"), who helped mold her onstage persona.[[36]](#cite_note-37) The pair began performing at downtown club venues like the [Mercury Lounge](/wiki/Mercury_Lounge "Mercury Lounge"), [the Bitter End](/wiki/The_Bitter_End "The Bitter End"), and the [Rockwood Music Hall](/wiki/Rockwood_Music_Hall "Rockwood Music Hall"). Their live performance art piece, known as "Lady Gaga and the Starlight Revue" and billed as "The Ultimate Pop Burlesque Rockshow", was a tribute to 1970s variety acts.[[37]](#cite_note-seattleweekly-38)[[38]](#cite_note-39) They performed at the 2007 [Lollapalooza](/wiki/Lollapalooza "Lollapalooza") music festival.[[37]](#cite_note-seattleweekly-38)

Having initially focused on [avant-garde](/wiki/Avant-garde "Avant-garde") [electronic dance music](/wiki/Electronic_dance_music "Electronic dance music"), Gaga began to incorporate pop melodies and the [glam rock](/wiki/Glam_rock "Glam rock") style of [David Bowie](/wiki/David_Bowie "David Bowie") and Queen into her songs. While Gaga and Starlight were performing, Fusari continued to develop the songs he had created with her, sending them to the producer and [record executive](/wiki/Music_executive "Music executive") [Vincent Herbert](/wiki/Vincent_Herbert "Vincent Herbert").[[39]](#cite_note-trans26-40) In November 2007, Herbert signed Gaga to his label Streamline Records, an [imprint](/wiki/Trade_name "Trade name") of [Interscope Records](/wiki/Interscope_Records "Interscope Records"), established that month.[[40]](#cite_note-newimprent-41) Gaga later credited Herbert as the man who discovered her.[[41]](#cite_note-vincentdiscover-42) Having served as an apprentice songwriter during an internship at [Famous Music](/wiki/Famous_Music "Famous Music") Publishing, Gaga struck a [music publishing](/wiki/Music_publisher_(popular_music) "Music publisher (popular music)") deal with [Sony/ATV Music Publishing](/wiki/Sony/ATV_Music_Publishing "Sony/ATV Music Publishing"). As a result, she was hired to write songs for [Britney Spears](/wiki/Britney_Spears "Britney Spears"), [New Kids on the Block](/wiki/New_Kids_on_the_Block "New Kids on the Block"), [Fergie](/wiki/Fergie_(singer) "Fergie (singer)"), and [the Pussycat Dolls](/wiki/The_Pussycat_Dolls "The Pussycat Dolls").[[42]](#cite_note-bbcover-43) At Interscope, musician [Akon](/wiki/Akon "Akon") was impressed with her singing abilities when she sang a [reference vocal](/wiki/Scratch_vocal "Scratch vocal") for one of his tracks in studio.[[43]](#cite_note-totallygaga-44) Akon convinced [Jimmy Iovine](/wiki/Jimmy_Iovine "Jimmy Iovine"), chairman and CEO of [Interscope Geffen A&M Records](/wiki/Interscope_Geffen_A%26M_Records "Interscope Geffen A&M Records") (a brother company for Def Jam), to form a joint deal by having Gaga also sign with his own label [KonLive Distribution](/wiki/KonLive_Distribution "KonLive Distribution"), making her his "franchise player".[[34]](#cite_note-Allmusic_bio-35)[[44]](#cite_note-franchise-45)

In late 2007, Gaga met with songwriter and producer [RedOne](/wiki/RedOne "RedOne").[[45]](#cite_note-hitquarters.com-46) She collaborated with him in the recording studio for a week on her debut album, signing with [Cherrytree Records](/wiki/Cherrytree_Records "Cherrytree Records"), an Interscope imprint established by producer and songwriter [Martin Kierszenbaum](/wiki/Martin_Kierszenbaum "Martin Kierszenbaum"); she also wrote four songs with Kierszenbaum.[[42]](#cite_note-bbcover-43) Despite securing a record deal, she said that some radio stations found her music too "racy", "dance-oriented", and "underground" for the mainstream market, to which she replied: "My name is Lady Gaga, I've been on the music scene for years, and I'm telling you, this is what's next."[[7]](#cite_note-independent-8)

### 2008–2010: Breakthrough with *The Fame* and *The Fame Monster*

By 2008, Gaga had relocated to Los Angeles to work extensively with her record label to complete her debut album, *[The Fame](/wiki/The_Fame "The Fame")*, and to set up her own creative team called the Haus of Gaga, modeled on [Andy Warhol](/wiki/Andy_Warhol "Andy Warhol")'s [The Factory](/wiki/The_Factory "The Factory").[[46]](#cite_note-Contactmusic.com-47)[[47]](#cite_note-48) *The Fame* was released on August 19, 2008,[[48]](#cite_note-49) and reached number one in Austria, Canada, Germany, Ireland, Switzerland and the UK, as well as the top five in Australia and the US.[[49]](#cite_note-50)[[50]](#cite_note-51) Its first two singles, "[Just Dance](/wiki/Just_Dance_(song) "Just Dance (song)")" and "[Poker Face](/wiki/Poker_Face_(song) "Poker Face (song)")",[[51]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEGray_II20123-52) reached number one in the United States,[[52]](#cite_note-USsongpeaks-53) Australia,[[53]](#cite_note-Australia-54) Canada[[54]](#cite_note-Canada-55) and the UK.[[55]](#cite_note-ukchart-56) The latter was also the [world's best-selling single](/wiki/List_of_best-selling_singles#Best-selling_singles_by_year_worldwide "List of best-selling singles") of 2009, with 9.8 million copies sold that year, and spent a record 83 weeks on *[Billboard](/wiki/Billboard_(magazine) "Billboard (magazine)")* magazine's [Digital Songs](/wiki/Digital_Songs "Digital Songs") chart.[[56]](#cite_note-57)[[57]](#cite_note-58) Three other singles, "[Eh, Eh (Nothing Else I Can Say)](/wiki/Eh,_Eh_(Nothing_Else_I_Can_Say) "Eh, Eh (Nothing Else I Can Say)")", "[LoveGame](/wiki/LoveGame "LoveGame")" and "[Paparazzi](/wiki/Paparazzi_(Lady_Gaga_song) "Paparazzi (Lady Gaga song)")", were released from the album;[[58]](#cite_note-59) the lattermost reached number one in Germany.[[59]](#cite_note-Germany-60) Remixed versions of the singles from *The Fame*, except "Eh, Eh (Nothing Else I Can Say)", were included on *[Hitmixes](/wiki/Hitmixes "Hitmixes")* in August 2009.[[60]](#cite_note-61) At the [52nd Annual Grammy Awards](/wiki/52nd_Annual_Grammy_Awards "52nd Annual Grammy Awards"), *The Fame* and "Poker Face" won [Best Dance/Electronic Album](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Best_Dance/Electronic_Album "Grammy Award for Best Dance/Electronic Album") and [Best Dance Recording](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Best_Dance_Recording "Grammy Award for Best Dance Recording"), respectively.[[61]](#cite_note-62)

[![A young woman on-stage wearing a studded leather bra](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d5/Lady_Gaga_-_The_Monster_Ball_Tour_-_Burswood_Dome_Perth_%284482953251%29.jpg/250px-Lady_Gaga_-_The_Monster_Ball_Tour_-_Burswood_Dome_Perth_%284482953251%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Lady_Gaga_-_The_Monster_Ball_Tour_-_Burswood_Dome_Perth_(4482953251).jpg)

Gaga on [The Monster Ball Tour](/wiki/The_Monster_Ball_Tour "The Monster Ball Tour") in 2010; it grossed $227 million and became the highest-grossing concert tour for a debut headlining artist[[62]](#cite_note-guardian-63)

Following her opening act on [the Pussycat Dolls](/wiki/The_Pussycat_Dolls "The Pussycat Dolls")' 2009 [Doll Domination Tour](/wiki/Doll_Domination_Tour "Doll Domination Tour") in Europe and Oceania, Gaga headlined her worldwide [The Fame Ball Tour](/wiki/The_Fame_Ball_Tour "The Fame Ball Tour"), which ran from March to September 2009.[[63]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEMorgan2010131-64) While traveling the globe, she wrote eight songs for *[The Fame Monster](/wiki/The_Fame_Monster "The Fame Monster")*, a reissue of *The Fame*.[[64]](#cite_note-65) Those new songs were also released as a standalone [EP](/wiki/Extended_play "Extended play") on November 18, 2009.[[65]](#cite_note-66) Its first single, "[Bad Romance](/wiki/Bad_Romance "Bad Romance")", was released one month earlier[[66]](#cite_note-67) and went number one in Canada[[54]](#cite_note-Canada-55) and the UK,[[55]](#cite_note-ukchart-56) and number two in the US,[[52]](#cite_note-USsongpeaks-53) Australia[[67]](#cite_note-68) and New Zealand.[[68]](#cite_note-69) "[Telephone](/wiki/Telephone_(song) "Telephone (song)")", with [Beyoncé](/wiki/Beyonc%C3%A9 "Beyoncé"), followed as the second single from the EP and became Gaga's fourth UK number one.[[69]](#cite_note-70)[[70]](#cite_note-71) Its third single was "[Alejandro](/wiki/Alejandro_(song) "Alejandro (song)")",[[71]](#cite_note-72) which reached number one in Finland[[72]](#cite_note-73) and attracted controversy when its music video was deemed [blasphemous](/wiki/Blasphemy "Blasphemy") by the [Catholic League](/wiki/Catholic_League_(U.S.) "Catholic League (U.S.)").[[73]](#cite_note-74) Both tracks reached the top five in the US.[[52]](#cite_note-USsongpeaks-53) The video for "Bad Romance" became the [most watched](/wiki/List_of_most-viewed_YouTube_videos "List of most-viewed YouTube videos") on YouTube in April 2010, and that October, Gaga became the first person with more than one billion combined views.[[74]](#cite_note-75)[[75]](#cite_note-76)

At the [2010 MTV Video Music Awards](/wiki/2010_MTV_Video_Music_Awards "2010 MTV Video Music Awards"), she won eight awards from 13 nominations, including [Video of the Year](/wiki/MTV_Video_Music_Award_for_Video_of_the_Year "MTV Video Music Award for Video of the Year") for "Bad Romance".[[76]](#cite_note-77) She was the most nominated artist for a single year, and the first woman to receive two nominations for Video of the Year at the same ceremony.[[77]](#cite_note-78) *The Fame Monster* won the [Grammy Award for Best Pop Vocal Album](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Best_Pop_Vocal_Album "Grammy Award for Best Pop Vocal Album"), and "Bad Romance" won [Best Female Pop Vocal Performance](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Best_Female_Pop_Vocal_Performance "Grammy Award for Best Female Pop Vocal Performance") and [Best Short Form Music Video](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Best_Short_Form_Music_Video "Grammy Award for Best Short Form Music Video") at the [53rd Annual Grammy Awards](/wiki/53rd_Annual_Grammy_Awards "53rd Annual Grammy Awards").[[78]](#cite_note-79) *[Rolling Stone](/wiki/Rolling_Stone "Rolling Stone")* featured "Bad Romance" and its music video on their respective lists of the "[500 Greatest Songs of All Time](/wiki/Rolling_Stone%27s_500_Greatest_Songs_of_All_Time "Rolling Stone's 500 Greatest Songs of All Time")" and "[100 Greatest Music Videos of All Time](/wiki/Rolling_Stone%27s_100_Greatest_Music_Videos_of_All_Time "Rolling Stone's 100 Greatest Music Videos of All Time")" in 2021.[[79]](#cite_note-80)[[80]](#cite_note-81)

In 2009, Gaga spent a record 150 weeks on the [UK Singles Chart](/wiki/UK_Singles_Chart "UK Singles Chart") and became the most downloaded female act in a year in the US, with 11.1 million downloads sold, earning an entry in the *[Guinness Book of World Records](/wiki/Guinness_Book_of_World_Records "Guinness Book of World Records")*.[[81]](#cite_note-82)[[82]](#cite_note-83) Worldwide, *The Fame* and *The Fame Monster* together have sold more than 15 million copies, and the latter was 2010's second best-selling album.[[83]](#cite_note-84)[[84]](#cite_note-85)[[85]](#cite_note-86) Its success allowed Gaga to start her second worldwide concert tour, [The Monster Ball Tour](/wiki/The_Monster_Ball_Tour "The Monster Ball Tour"), and release *[The Remix](/wiki/The_Remix_(Lady_Gaga_album) "The Remix (Lady Gaga album)")*, her final record with Cherrytree Records[[86]](#cite_note-87) and [among the best-selling remix albums of all time](/wiki/List_of_best-selling_remix_albums "List of best-selling remix albums").[[87]](#cite_note-88)[[88]](#cite_note-bbannounceball-89) The Monster Ball Tour ran from November 2009 to May 2011 and grossed $227.4 million, making it the highest-grossing concert tour for a debut headlining artist.[[62]](#cite_note-guardian-63)[[89]](#cite_note-90) Concerts performed at [Madison Square Garden](/wiki/Madison_Square_Garden "Madison Square Garden") in New York City were filmed for an [HBO](/wiki/HBO "HBO") television special, *[Lady Gaga Presents the Monster Ball Tour: At Madison Square Garden](/wiki/Lady_Gaga_Presents_the_Monster_Ball_Tour:_At_Madison_Square_Garden "Lady Gaga Presents the Monster Ball Tour: At Madison Square Garden")*.[[90]](#cite_note-91) Gaga also performed songs from her albums at the 2009 *[Royal Variety Performance](/wiki/Royal_Variety_Performance "Royal Variety Performance")*, the 52nd Annual Grammy Awards, and the [2010 Brit Awards](/wiki/2010_Brit_Awards "2010 Brit Awards").[[91]](#cite_note-92) Before [Michael Jackson](/wiki/Michael_Jackson "Michael Jackson")'s [death](/wiki/Death_of_Michael_Jackson "Death of Michael Jackson"), Gaga was set to take part in his canceled [This Is It](/wiki/This_Is_It_(concert_residency) "This Is It (concert residency)") concert series at the [O2 Arena](/wiki/The_O2_Arena_(London) "The O2 Arena (London)") in the UK.[[92]](#cite_note-93)

During this era, Gaga ventured into business, collaborating with consumer electronics company [Monster Cable Products](/wiki/Monster_Cable_Products "Monster Cable Products") to create in-ear, jewel-encrusted headphones called Heartbeats by Lady Gaga.[[93]](#cite_note-94) She also partnered with [Polaroid](/wiki/Polaroid_Corporation "Polaroid Corporation") in January 2010 as their creative director and announced a suite of photo-capture products called Grey Label.[[94]](#cite_note-95)[[95]](#cite_note-96) Her collaboration with her past record producer and ex-boyfriend Rob Fusari led to a lawsuit against her production team, Mermaid Music LLC.[[b]](#cite_note-99) At this time, Gaga was tested borderline positive for [lupus](/wiki/Lupus "Lupus") but claimed not to be affected by the symptoms and hoped to maintain a healthy lifestyle.[[98]](#cite_note-comeparty-100)[[99]](#cite_note-101)

### 2011–2014: *Born This Way*, *Artpop*, and *Cheek to Cheek*

In February 2011, Gaga released "[Born This Way](/wiki/Born_This_Way_(song) "Born This Way (song)")", the lead single from her [studio album of the same name](/wiki/Born_This_Way_(album) "Born This Way (album)"). The song sold more than one million copies within five days, earning the *Guinness World Record* for the fastest selling single on [iTunes](/wiki/ITunes "ITunes").[[100]](#cite_note-champ-102) It debuted atop the [*Billboard* Hot 100](/wiki/Billboard_Hot_100 "Billboard Hot 100"), becoming the 1,000th number-one single in the history of the charts.[[101]](#cite_note-103) Its second single "[Judas](/wiki/Judas_(Lady_Gaga_song) "Judas (Lady Gaga song)")" followed two months later,[[102]](#cite_note-dwn-104) and "[The Edge of Glory](/wiki/The_Edge_of_Glory "The Edge of Glory")" served as its third single.[[103]](#cite_note-105) Both reached the top 10 in the US and the UK.[[52]](#cite_note-USsongpeaks-53)[[55]](#cite_note-ukchart-56) Her music video for "The Edge of Glory", unlike her previous work, portrays her dancing on a fire escape and walking on a lonely street, without intricate choreography and back-up dancers.[[104]](#cite_note-106)

[![Gaga performing onstage wearing black studded jacket and bodysuit.](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f0/Lady_Gaga_The_Edge_of_Glory_GMA2_%28cropped%29.jpg/250px-Lady_Gaga_The_Edge_of_Glory_GMA2_%28cropped%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Lady_Gaga_The_Edge_of_Glory_GMA2_(cropped).jpg)

Gaga promoting *[Born This Way](/wiki/Born_This_Way_(album) "Born This Way (album)")* with a performance on *[Good Morning America](/wiki/Good_Morning_America "Good Morning America")* in 2011

*Born This Way* was released on May 23, 2011,[[102]](#cite_note-dwn-104) and debuted atop the [*Billboard* 200](/wiki/Billboard_200 "Billboard 200") with first-week sales of [1.1 million copies](/wiki/Lists_of_fastest-selling_albums "Lists of fastest-selling albums").[[105]](#cite_note-107) It sold eight million copies worldwide and received three Grammy nominations, including Gaga's third consecutive nomination for [Album of the Year](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Album_of_the_Year "Grammy Award for Album of the Year").[[106]](#cite_note-108)[[107]](#cite_note-109) *Rolling Stone* listed the record among "[The 500 Greatest Albums of All Time](/wiki/Rolling_Stone%27s_500_Greatest_Albums_of_All_Time "Rolling Stone's 500 Greatest Albums of All Time")" in 2020.[[108]](#cite_note-110) *Born This Way*'s following singles were "[You and I](/wiki/You_and_I_(Lady_Gaga_song) "You and I (Lady Gaga song)")" and "[Marry the Night](/wiki/Marry_the_Night "Marry the Night")",[[109]](#cite_note-youandimv-111) which reached numbers 6 and 29 in the US, respectively.[[52]](#cite_note-USsongpeaks-53) Its track, "[Bloody Mary](/wiki/Bloody_Mary_(song) "Bloody Mary (song)")", became a resurgent success and was released as a single in 2022.[[110]](#cite_note-112) While filming the "You and I" music video, Gaga met and started dating actor [Taylor Kinney](/wiki/Taylor_Kinney "Taylor Kinney") in July 2011, who played her love interest.[[111]](#cite_note-113)[[112]](#cite_note-114) She also embarked on the [Born This Way Ball](/wiki/Born_This_Way_Ball "Born This Way Ball") tour in April 2012, which was scheduled to conclude the following March, but ended one month earlier when Gaga canceled the remaining dates due to a [labral](/wiki/Acetabular_labrum "Acetabular labrum") tear of her right hip that required surgery.[[113]](#cite_note-115) While refunds for the cancellations were estimated to be worth $25 million,[[114]](#cite_note-116) the tour grossed $183.9 million globally.[[115]](#cite_note-117)

In 2011, Gaga also worked with [Tony Bennett](/wiki/Tony_Bennett "Tony Bennett") on a [jazz](/wiki/Jazz "Jazz") version of "[The Lady Is a Tramp](/wiki/The_Lady_Is_a_Tramp "The Lady Is a Tramp")",[[116]](#cite_note-118) with [Elton John](/wiki/Elton_John "Elton John") on "Hello Hello" for the animated feature film *[Gnomeo & Juliet](/wiki/Gnomeo_%26_Juliet "Gnomeo & Juliet")*,[[117]](#cite_note-119) and with [The Lonely Island](/wiki/The_Lonely_Island "The Lonely Island") and [Justin Timberlake](/wiki/Justin_Timberlake "Justin Timberlake") on "[3-Way (The Golden Rule)](/wiki/3-Way_(The_Golden_Rule) "3-Way (The Golden Rule)")".[[118]](#cite_note-120) She also performed a concert at the [Sydney Town Hall](/wiki/Sydney_Town_Hall "Sydney Town Hall") in Australia that year to promote *Born This Way* and to celebrate former US President [Bill Clinton](/wiki/Bill_Clinton "Bill Clinton")'s 65th birthday.[[119]](#cite_note-121) In November, she was featured in a [Thanksgiving](/wiki/Thanksgiving_(United_States) "Thanksgiving (United States)") television special titled *[A Very Gaga Thanksgiving](/wiki/A_Very_Gaga_Thanksgiving "A Very Gaga Thanksgiving")*, which attracted 5.7 million American viewers and spawned the release of her fourth EP, *[A Very Gaga Holiday](/wiki/A_Very_Gaga_Holiday "A Very Gaga Holiday")*.[[120]](#cite_note-ratings2-122) In 2012, Gaga guest-starred as an animated version of herself in an episode of *[The Simpsons](/wiki/The_Simpsons "The Simpsons")* called "[Lisa Goes Gaga](/wiki/Lisa_Goes_Gaga "Lisa Goes Gaga")",[[121]](#cite_note-123) and released her first fragrance, [Lady Gaga Fame](/wiki/Lady_Gaga_Fame "Lady Gaga Fame"), followed by a second one, [Eau de Gaga](/wiki/Eau_de_Gaga "Eau de Gaga"), in 2014.[[c]](#cite_note-126)

Gaga began work on her third studio album, *[Artpop](/wiki/Artpop "Artpop")*, in early 2012, during the Born This Way Ball tour. She crafted the album to mirror "a night at the club".[[124]](#cite_note-127)[[125]](#cite_note-128)[[126]](#cite_note-mtv222-129) In August 2013, Gaga released the album's lead single "[Applause](/wiki/Applause_(Lady_Gaga_song) "Applause (Lady Gaga song)")",[[127]](#cite_note-130) which reached number one in Hungary, number four in the US, and number five in the UK.[[55]](#cite_note-ukchart-56)[[52]](#cite_note-USsongpeaks-53)[[128]](#cite_note-131) A [lyric video](/wiki/Lyric_video "Lyric video") for *Artpop* track "[Aura](/wiki/Aura_(Lady_Gaga_song) "Aura (Lady Gaga song)")" followed in October to accompany [Robert Rodriguez](/wiki/Robert_Rodriguez "Robert Rodriguez")'s *[Machete Kills](/wiki/Machete_Kills "Machete Kills")*, where she plays an assassin named La Chameleon.[[129]](#cite_note-132) The film received generally mixed reviews and earned less than half of its $33 million budget.[[130]](#cite_note-133)[[131]](#cite_note-134) The second *Artpop* single, "[Do What U Want](/wiki/Do_What_U_Want "Do What U Want")", featured singer [R. Kelly](/wiki/R._Kelly "R. Kelly") and was released later that month,[[132]](#cite_note-dowhatSingleAnnouncement-135) topping the charts in Hungary and reaching number 13 in the US.[[52]](#cite_note-USsongpeaks-53)[[133]](#cite_note-136) *Artpop* was released on November 6, 2013, to mixed reviews.[[134]](#cite_note-137) Helen Brown of *[The Daily Telegraph](/wiki/The_Daily_Telegraph "The Daily Telegraph")* criticized Gaga for making another album about her fame and doubted the record's originality, but found it "great for dancing".[[135]](#cite_note-telegraph-138) The album debuted atop the *Billboard* 200 chart, and sold more than 2.5 million copies worldwide as of July 2014.[[136]](#cite_note-139)[[137]](#cite_note-140) "[G.U.Y.](/wiki/G.U.Y. "G.U.Y.")" was released as the third single in March 2014 and peaked at number 76 in the US.[[52]](#cite_note-USsongpeaks-53)[[138]](#cite_note-radioit-141)

[![A man and a woman standing closely together. The man (left) is wearing a gray suit, white shirt and a black tie while the woman (right) is wearing a black gown, black gloves and a black headpiece. They both hold a microphone in their left hand.](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/3a/Tony_Bennett_%26_Lady_GaGa%2C_Cheek_to_Cheek_Tour_04_%28cropped%29.jpg/250px-Tony_Bennett_%26_Lady_GaGa%2C_Cheek_to_Cheek_Tour_04_%28cropped%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Tony_Bennett_%26_Lady_GaGa,_Cheek_to_Cheek_Tour_04_(cropped).jpg)

With the *[Cheek to Cheek](/wiki/Cheek_to_Cheek_(album) "Cheek to Cheek (album)")* era, Gaga (seen here performing on the [Cheek to Cheek Tour](/wiki/Cheek_to_Cheek_Tour "Cheek to Cheek Tour") alongside [Tony Bennett](/wiki/Tony_Bennett "Tony Bennett")) ushered in an overhaul of her image[[139]](#cite_note-reinvention-142)

Gaga hosted an episode of *[Saturday Night Live](/wiki/Saturday_Night_Live "Saturday Night Live")* in November 2013.[[140]](#cite_note-Nov2013SNL-143) After holding her second Thanksgiving Day television special on [ABC](/wiki/American_Broadcasting_Company "American Broadcasting Company"), *[Lady Gaga and the Muppets Holiday Spectacular](/wiki/Lady_Gaga_and_the_Muppets_Holiday_Spectacular "Lady Gaga and the Muppets Holiday Spectacular")*, she performed a special rendition of "Do What U Want" with [Christina Aguilera](/wiki/Christina_Aguilera "Christina Aguilera") on the [fifth season](/wiki/The_Voice_(U.S._season_5) "The Voice (U.S. season 5)") of the American reality talent show *[The Voice](/wiki/The_Voice_(U.S._TV_series) "The Voice (U.S. TV series)")*.[[141]](#cite_note-Thanksgiving2013-144)[[142]](#cite_note-145) In March 2014, Gaga had a [seven-day concert residency](/wiki/Lady_Gaga_Live_at_Roseland_Ballroom "Lady Gaga Live at Roseland Ballroom") commemorating the last performance at New York's [Roseland Ballroom](/wiki/Roseland_Ballroom "Roseland Ballroom") before its closure.[[143]](#cite_note-146) Two months later, she embarked on the [ArtRave: The Artpop Ball](/wiki/ArtRave:_The_Artpop_Ball "ArtRave: The Artpop Ball") tour, building on concepts from her [ArtRave](/wiki/ArtRave "ArtRave") promotional event. Earning $83 million, the tour included cities canceled from the Born This Way Ball tour itinerary.[[144]](#cite_note-147) In the meantime, Gaga split from longtime manager [Troy Carter](/wiki/Troy_Carter_(music_industry) "Troy Carter (music industry)") over "creative differences",[[145]](#cite_note-NY_news-148) and by June 2014, she and new manager Bobby Campbell joined Artist Nation, the artist management division of [Live Nation Entertainment](/wiki/Live_Nation_Entertainment "Live Nation Entertainment").[[146]](#cite_note-149) She briefly appeared in Rodriguez's *[Sin City: A Dame to Kill For](/wiki/Sin_City:_A_Dame_to_Kill_For "Sin City: A Dame to Kill For")*, and was confirmed as [Versace](/wiki/Versace "Versace")'s spring-summer 2014 ambassador with a campaign called "Lady Gaga For Versace".[[147]](#cite_note-GagaNailedIt-150)[[148]](#cite_note-151)

In September 2014, Gaga released a collaborative jazz album with Tony Bennett titled *[Cheek to Cheek](/wiki/Cheek_to_Cheek_(album) "Cheek to Cheek (album)")*. The inspiration behind the album came from her friendship with Bennett, and fascination with jazz music since her childhood.[[149]](#cite_note-press-152) He stated that Gaga is "the most talented artist I have ever met".[[150]](#cite_note-153) Before the album was released, it produced the singles "[Anything Goes](/wiki/Anything_Goes_(Cole_Porter_song)#Tony_Bennett_and_Lady_Gaga_version "Anything Goes (Cole Porter song)")" and "[I Can't Give You Anything but Love](/wiki/I_Can%27t_Give_You_Anything_but_Love,_Baby#Tony_Bennett_and_Lady_Gaga_version "I Can't Give You Anything but Love, Baby")".[[151]](#cite_note-154) *Cheek to Cheek* received generally favorable reviews;[[152]](#cite_note-mc-155) *The Guardian*'s Caroline Sullivan praised Gaga's vocals and Howard Reich of the *[Chicago Tribune](/wiki/Chicago_Tribune "Chicago Tribune")* wrote that "*Cheek to Cheek* serves up the real thing, start to finish".[[153]](#cite_note-guarrev-156)[[154]](#cite_note-157) The record was Gaga's third consecutive number-one album on the *Billboard* 200,[[155]](#cite_note-usc2c-158) and won a [Grammy Award for Best Traditional Pop Vocal Album](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Best_Traditional_Pop_Vocal_Album "Grammy Award for Best Traditional Pop Vocal Album").[[156]](#cite_note-159) The duo recorded the concert special *[Tony Bennett and Lady Gaga: Cheek to Cheek Live!](/wiki/Tony_Bennett_and_Lady_Gaga:_Cheek_to_Cheek_Live! "Tony Bennett and Lady Gaga: Cheek to Cheek Live!")*,[[157]](#cite_note-160) and embarked on the [Cheek to Cheek Tour](/wiki/Cheek_to_Cheek_Tour "Cheek to Cheek Tour") from December 2014 to August 2015.[[158]](#cite_note-161)

### 2015–2017: *American Horror Story*, *Joanne*, and Super Bowl performances

In February 2015, Gaga became engaged to Taylor Kinney.[[159]](#cite_note-162) After the lukewarm response to *Artpop*, Gaga began to reinvent her image and style. According to *Billboard*, this shift started with the release of *Cheek to Cheek* and the attention she received for her performance at the [87th Academy Awards](/wiki/87th_Academy_Awards "87th Academy Awards"), where she sang a medley of songs from *[The Sound of Music](/wiki/The_Sound_of_Music_(film) "The Sound of Music (film)")* in a tribute to [Julie Andrews](/wiki/Julie_Andrews "Julie Andrews").[[139]](#cite_note-reinvention-142) Considered one of her best performances by *Billboard*, it triggered more than 214,000 interactions per minute globally on Facebook.[[160]](#cite_note-163)[[161]](#cite_note-164) She and [Diane Warren](/wiki/Diane_Warren "Diane Warren") co-wrote the song "[Til It Happens to You](/wiki/Til_It_Happens_to_You "Til It Happens to You")" for the documentary *[The Hunting Ground](/wiki/The_Hunting_Ground "The Hunting Ground")*, which earned them the [Satellite Award for Best Original Song](/wiki/Satellite_Award_for_Best_Original_Song "Satellite Award for Best Original Song") and an [Academy Award](/wiki/Academy_Award "Academy Award") nomination in [the same category](/wiki/Academy_Award_for_Best_Original_Song "Academy Award for Best Original Song").[[162]](#cite_note-165) *Billboard* named Gaga their 2015 [Woman of the Year](/wiki/Billboard_Women_in_Music "Billboard Women in Music").[[163]](#cite_note-166)

Gaga had spent much of her early life wanting to be an actress, and achieved her goal when she starred in *[American Horror Story: Hotel](/wiki/American_Horror_Story:_Hotel "American Horror Story: Hotel")*.[[164]](#cite_note-GoldenGlobeAHS-167) Running from October 2015 to January 2016, *Hotel* is the fifth season of the television [anthology](/wiki/Anthology_series "Anthology series") horror series, *[American Horror Story](/wiki/American_Horror_Story "American Horror Story")*, in which Gaga played a hotel owner named [Elizabeth](/wiki/List_of_American_Horror_Story:_Hotel_characters#The_Countess "List of American Horror Story: Hotel characters").[[165]](#cite_note-168)[[166]](#cite_note-169) At the [73rd Golden Globe Awards](/wiki/73rd_Golden_Globe_Awards "73rd Golden Globe Awards"), Gaga received the [Best Actress in a Miniseries or Television Film](/wiki/Golden_Globe_Award_for_Best_Actress_%E2%80%93_Miniseries_or_Television_Film "Golden Globe Award for Best Actress – Miniseries or Television Film") award for her work on the season.[[164]](#cite_note-GoldenGlobeAHS-167) She appeared in [Nick Knight](/wiki/Nick_Knight_(photographer) "Nick Knight (photographer)")'s 2015 fashion film for [Tom Ford](/wiki/Tom_Ford "Tom Ford")'s 2016 spring campaign[[167]](#cite_note-170) and was guest editor for *[V](/wiki/V_(American_magazine) "V (American magazine)")* fashion magazine's 99th issue in January 2016, which featured 16 different covers.[[168]](#cite_note-V_mag-171) She received Editor of the Year award at the [Fashion Los Angeles Awards](/wiki/Daily_Front_Row "Daily Front Row").[[169]](#cite_note-172)

[![Lady Gaga standing behind a microphone stand with a pink guitar in her hands, wearing black leather fringe](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/5a/Lady_Gaga_JWT_Toronto%2C_2017-09-06_%28cropped2%29.jpg/250px-Lady_Gaga_JWT_Toronto%2C_2017-09-06_%28cropped2%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Lady_Gaga_JWT_Toronto,_2017-09-06_(cropped2).jpg)

Gaga performing on the [Joanne World Tour](/wiki/Joanne_World_Tour "Joanne World Tour") in 2017

In February 2016, Gaga sang the [US national anthem](/wiki/The_Star-Spangled_Banner "The Star-Spangled Banner") at [Super Bowl 50](/wiki/Super_Bowl_50 "Super Bowl 50"),[[170]](#cite_note-173) partnered with [Intel](/wiki/Intel "Intel") and [Nile Rodgers](/wiki/Nile_Rodgers "Nile Rodgers") for a tribute performance to the late David Bowie at the [58th Annual Grammy Awards](/wiki/58th_Annual_Grammy_Awards "58th Annual Grammy Awards"),[[171]](#cite_note-174) and sang "Til It Happens to You" at the [88th Academy Awards](/wiki/88th_Academy_Awards "88th Academy Awards"), where she was introduced by [Joe Biden](/wiki/Joe_Biden "Joe Biden") and was accompanied on-stage by 50 people who had suffered from sexual assault.[[172]](#cite_note-175) Her engagement to Taylor Kinney ended in July; she later said her career had interfered with their relationship.[[173]](#cite_note-176)

Gaga played a witch named [Scathach](/wiki/Sc%C3%A1thach "Scáthach") in *[American Horror Story: Roanoke](/wiki/American_Horror_Story:_Roanoke "American Horror Story: Roanoke")*, the series' sixth season,[[174]](#cite_note-177) which ran from September to November 2016.[[175]](#cite_note-178)[[176]](#cite_note-179) Her role in the fifth season of the show ultimately influenced her future music, prompting her to feature "the art of darkness".[[177]](#cite_note-180) In September 2016, she released her fifth album's lead single, "[Perfect Illusion](/wiki/Perfect_Illusion "Perfect Illusion")", which topped the charts in France and reached number 15 in the US.[[178]](#cite_note-181)[[179]](#cite_note-182)[[180]](#cite_note-SuperBowlResult-183) The album, titled *[Joanne](/wiki/Joanne_(album) "Joanne (album)")*, was named after Gaga's late aunt, who was an inspiration for the music.[[181]](#cite_note-184) It was released on October 21, 2016, and became Gaga's fourth number one album on the *Billboard* 200, making her the first woman to reach the US chart's summit four times in the 2010s.[[182]](#cite_note-185) The album's second single, "[Million Reasons](/wiki/Million_Reasons "Million Reasons")", followed the next month and reached number four in the US.[[180]](#cite_note-SuperBowlResult-183)[[183]](#cite_note-186) She later released a piano version of the album's [title track](/wiki/Joanne_(Lady_Gaga_song) "Joanne (Lady Gaga song)") in 2018,[[184]](#cite_note-187) which won a [Grammy for Best Pop Solo Performance](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Best_Pop_Solo_Performance "Grammy Award for Best Pop Solo Performance").[[185]](#cite_note-2019Grammys-188) To promote the album, Gaga embarked on the three-date [Dive Bar Tour](/wiki/Dive_Bar_Tour_(Lady_Gaga) "Dive Bar Tour (Lady Gaga)").[[186]](#cite_note-189)

Gaga performed as the headlining act during the [Super Bowl LI halftime show](/wiki/Super_Bowl_LI_halftime_show "Super Bowl LI halftime show") on February 5, 2017. Her performance featured a group of hundreds of lighted drones forming various shapes in the sky above Houston's [NRG Stadium](/wiki/NRG_Stadium "NRG Stadium")—the first time robotic aircraft appeared in a Super Bowl program.[[187]](#cite_note-190) It attracted 117.5 million viewers in the United States, exceeding the game's 111.3 million viewers and making it the third most-watched Super Bowl halftime show at the time.[[188]](#cite_note-191) The performance led to a surge of 410,000 song downloads in the United States for Gaga and earned her an [Emmy](/wiki/Emmy_Award "Emmy Award") nomination in the [Outstanding Special Class Program](/wiki/Primetime_Emmy_Award_for_Outstanding_Special_Class_Program "Primetime Emmy Award for Outstanding Special Class Program") category.[[189]](#cite_note-192)[[190]](#cite_note-193) [CBS Sports](/wiki/CBS_Sports "CBS Sports") included her performance as the second best in the history of Super Bowl halftime shows.[[191]](#cite_note-194) In April, Gaga headlined the [Coachella Valley Music and Arts Festival](/wiki/Coachella_Valley_Music_and_Arts_Festival "Coachella Valley Music and Arts Festival").[[192]](#cite_note-Coachella-195) She also released a standalone single, "[The Cure](/wiki/The_Cure_(song) "The Cure (song)")", which reached the top 10 in Australia.[[193]](#cite_note-196)[[194]](#cite_note-197) Four months later, Gaga began the [Joanne World Tour](/wiki/Joanne_World_Tour "Joanne World Tour"), which she announced after the Super Bowl LI halftime show.[[195]](#cite_note-tickets-198) Gaga's creation of *Joanne* and preparation for her halftime show performance were featured in the documentary *[Gaga: Five Foot Two](/wiki/Gaga:_Five_Foot_Two "Gaga: Five Foot Two")*, which premiered on [Netflix](/wiki/Netflix "Netflix") that September.[[196]](#cite_note-199) Throughout the film, she was seen suffering from [chronic pain](/wiki/Chronic_pain "Chronic pain"), which was later revealed to be the effect of a long-term condition called [fibromyalgia](/wiki/Fibromyalgia "Fibromyalgia").[[197]](#cite_note-200) In February 2018, it prompted Gaga to cancel the last ten shows of the Joanne World Tour, which ultimately grossed $95 million from 842,000 tickets sold.[[198]](#cite_note-201)[[199]](#cite_note-202)

### 2018–2019: *A Star Is Born* and Las Vegas residency

[![A picture of Lady Gaga in a burgundy one-shoulder dress, looking to the right.](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/49/Lady_Gaga_at_the_2018_Toronto_International_Film_Festival_%28cropped_1%29.jpg/250px-Lady_Gaga_at_the_2018_Toronto_International_Film_Festival_%28cropped_1%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Lady_Gaga_at_the_2018_Toronto_International_Film_Festival_(cropped_1).jpg)

Gaga at the [2018 Toronto International Film Festival](/wiki/2018_Toronto_International_Film_Festival "2018 Toronto International Film Festival") prior to the screening of *[A Star Is Born](/wiki/A_Star_Is_Born_(2018_film) "A Star Is Born (2018 film)")*, which was her first lead role in a film[[200]](#cite_note-203)

In March 2018, Gaga released a cover of Elton John's "[Your Song](/wiki/Your_Song "Your Song")" for his tribute album *[Revamp](/wiki/Revamp_%26_Restoration#Revamp "Revamp & Restoration")*.[[201]](#cite_note-204) Later that year, she starred as struggling singer Ally in [Bradley Cooper](/wiki/Bradley_Cooper "Bradley Cooper")'s musical romantic drama *[A Star Is Born](/wiki/A_Star_Is_Born_(2018_film) "A Star Is Born (2018 film)")*, a remake of the [1937 film of the same name](/wiki/A_Star_Is_Born_(1937_film) "A Star Is Born (1937 film)"). The film follows Ally's relationship with singer Jackson Maine (played by Cooper), which becomes strained after her career begins to overshadow his. It received acclaim from critics, with a consensus that the movie had "appealing leads, deft direction, and an affecting love story".[[202]](#cite_note-205) Cooper approached Gaga after seeing her perform at a cancer research fundraiser. An admirer of Cooper's work, Gaga agreed to the project due to its portrayal of addiction and depression.[[203]](#cite_note-206)[[204]](#cite_note-207) *A Star Is Born* premiered at the [2018 Venice Film Festival](/wiki/75th_Venice_International_Film_Festival "75th Venice International Film Festival"), and was released worldwide that October.[[205]](#cite_note-208) Gaga's performance was acclaimed by film critics, with [Peter Bradshaw](/wiki/Peter_Bradshaw "Peter Bradshaw") of *[The Guardian](/wiki/The_Guardian "The Guardian")* labeling the film "outrageously watchable" and stating that "Gaga's ability to be part ordinary person, part extraterrestrial celebrity empress functions at the highest level".[[206]](#cite_note-209) [Stephanie Zacharek](/wiki/Stephanie_Zacharek "Stephanie Zacharek") of *[Time](/wiki/Time_(magazine) "Time (magazine)")* magazine similarly highlighted her "knockout performance" and found her to be "charismatic" without her usual makeup, wigs and costumes.[[207]](#cite_note-210) For the role, Gaga won the [National Board of Review](/wiki/National_Board_of_Review_Award_for_Best_Actress "National Board of Review Award for Best Actress") and [Critics' Choice](/wiki/Critics%27_Choice_Movie_Award_for_Best_Actress "Critics' Choice Movie Award for Best Actress") awards for Best Actress, in addition to receiving nominations for the [Academy Award](/wiki/Academy_Award_for_Best_Actress "Academy Award for Best Actress"), [Golden Globe Award](/wiki/Golden_Globe_Award_for_Best_Actress_in_a_Motion_Picture_%E2%80%93_Drama "Golden Globe Award for Best Actress in a Motion Picture – Drama"), [Screen Actors Guild Award](/wiki/Screen_Actors_Guild_Award_for_Outstanding_Performance_by_a_Female_Actor_in_a_Leading_Role "Screen Actors Guild Award for Outstanding Performance by a Female Actor in a Leading Role") and [BAFTA Award](/wiki/BAFTA_Award_for_Best_Actress_in_a_Leading_Role "BAFTA Award for Best Actress in a Leading Role") for Best Actress.[[208]](#cite_note-a_star_is_born_awards_and_nominations-211)

Gaga and Cooper co-wrote and produced most of the songs on the [soundtrack for *A Star Is Born*](/wiki/A_Star_Is_Born_(2018_soundtrack) "A Star Is Born (2018 soundtrack)"), which she insisted they perform live in the film.[[209]](#cite_note-212) Its lead single, "[Shallow](/wiki/Shallow_(Lady_Gaga_and_Bradley_Cooper_song) "Shallow (Lady Gaga and Bradley Cooper song)")", performed by the two, was released on September 27, 2018,[[210]](#cite_note-213) and topped the charts in various countries including Australia, the UK and the US.[[211]](#cite_note-214) The soundtrack contains 34 tracks, including 17 original songs, and received generally positive reviews;[[212]](#cite_note-215) Mark Kennedy of *[The Washington Post](/wiki/The_Washington_Post "The Washington Post")* called it a "five-star marvel" and Ben Beaumont-Thomas of *The Guardian* termed it an "instant classics full of Gaga's emotional might".[[213]](#cite_note-TWP-216)[[214]](#cite_note-217) Commercially, the soundtrack debuted at number one in the US, extending Gaga's record for the most *Billboard* 200 number ones in the 2010s among women.[[215]](#cite_note-218) It additionally topped the charts in Australia, Canada, Ireland, New Zealand, Switzerland and the UK.[[216]](#cite_note-219) As of June 2019, the soundtrack had sold over six million copies worldwide.[[217]](#cite_note-220) The album won Gaga four Grammy Awards—[Best Compilation Soundtrack for Visual Media](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Best_Compilation_Soundtrack_for_Visual_Media "Grammy Award for Best Compilation Soundtrack for Visual Media") and [Best Pop Duo/Group Performance](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Best_Pop_Duo/Group_Performance "Grammy Award for Best Pop Duo/Group Performance") and [Best Song Written for Visual Media](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Best_Song_Written_for_Visual_Media "Grammy Award for Best Song Written for Visual Media") for "Shallow", as well as the latter category for "[I'll Never Love Again](/wiki/I%27ll_Never_Love_Again "I'll Never Love Again")"—and a [BAFTA Award for Best Film Music](/wiki/BAFTA_Award_for_Best_Film_Music "BAFTA Award for Best Film Music").[[185]](#cite_note-2019Grammys-188)[[218]](#cite_note-221)[[219]](#cite_note-222) "Shallow" also won her the Academy Award, [Golden Globe Award](/wiki/Golden_Globe_Award_for_Best_Original_Song "Golden Globe Award for Best Original Song"), [Critics' Choice Award](/wiki/Critics%27_Choice_Movie_Award_for_Best_Song "Critics' Choice Movie Award for Best Song"), and Satellite Award for Best Original Song.[[208]](#cite_note-a_star_is_born_awards_and_nominations-211) Gaga gave live performances of the song at the [61st Annual Grammy Awards](/wiki/61st_Annual_Grammy_Awards "61st Annual Grammy Awards") and the [91st Academy Awards](/wiki/91st_Academy_Awards "91st Academy Awards").[[220]](#cite_note-223)[[221]](#cite_note-224)

In October, Gaga announced her engagement to talent agent Christian Carino whom she had met in early 2017.[[222]](#cite_note-225) They ended the engagement in February 2019.[[223]](#cite_note-226) Gaga signed a [concert residency](/wiki/Concert_residency "Concert residency"), named [Lady Gaga Enigma + Jazz & Piano](/wiki/Lady_Gaga_Enigma_%2B_Jazz_%26_Piano "Lady Gaga Enigma + Jazz & Piano"), to perform at the [MGM Park Theater](/wiki/Monte_Carlo_Resort_and_Casino "Monte Carlo Resort and Casino") in [Las Vegas](/wiki/Las_Vegas "Las Vegas").[[224]](#cite_note-ewenigma-227) The residency consisted of two types of shows: Enigma, which focused on theatricality and included Gaga's biggest hits,[[225]](#cite_note-228) and Jazz & Piano, which involved tracks from the [Great American Songbook](/wiki/Great_American_Songbook "Great American Songbook") and stripped-down versions of Gaga's songs. The Enigma show opened in December 2018 and the Jazz & Piano in January 2019.[[226]](#cite_note-229) Gaga launched her [vegan](/wiki/Vegan "Vegan") makeup line, [Haus Laboratories](/wiki/Haus_Labs "Haus Labs"), in September 2019 exclusively on [Amazon](/wiki/Amazon_(company) "Amazon (company)"). Consisting of 40 products, including liquid eyeliners, lip glosses and face mask sticker, it reached number one on Amazon's list of best-selling lipsticks.[[227]](#cite_note-230)

### 2020–2023: *Chromatica*, *Love for Sale*, and *House of Gucci*

Gaga's sixth studio album, *[Chromatica](/wiki/Chromatica "Chromatica")*, was released on May 29, 2020, to positive reviews.[[228]](#cite_note-231)[[229]](#cite_note-232) It debuted atop the US charts, becoming her sixth consecutive number-one album in the country, and reached the top spot in more than a dozen other territories including Australia, Canada, France, Italy and the UK.[[230]](#cite_note-233) *Chromatica*'s lead single, "[Stupid Love](/wiki/Stupid_Love_(Lady_Gaga_song) "Stupid Love (Lady Gaga song)")", was released on February 28, 2020,[[231]](#cite_note-234) and charted at number five in the US and the UK.[[232]](#cite_note-235) The second single, "[Rain on Me](/wiki/Rain_on_Me_(Lady_Gaga_and_Ariana_Grande_song) "Rain on Me (Lady Gaga and Ariana Grande song)")" with [Ariana Grande](/wiki/Ariana_Grande "Ariana Grande"), followed on May 22.[[233]](#cite_note-236) It won the Best Pop Duo/Group Performance at the [63rd Annual Grammy Awards](/wiki/63rd_Annual_Grammy_Awards "63rd Annual Grammy Awards"), and debuted at number one in the US, making Gaga the third person to top the country's chart in the 2000s, 2010s and 2020s.[[234]](#cite_note-237)[[235]](#cite_note-238) At the [2020 MTV Video Music Awards](/wiki/2020_MTV_Video_Music_Awards "2020 MTV Video Music Awards"), Gaga won five awards, including the inaugural Tricon Award representing achievement in three (or more) fields of entertainment.[[236]](#cite_note-mtv_vma-239) In September 2020, she appeared in the video campaign for [Valentino](/wiki/Valentino_(fashion_house) "Valentino (fashion house)")'s Voce Viva fragrance, singing a stripped-down version of *Chromatica* track "[Sine from Above](/wiki/Sine_from_Above "Sine from Above")", along with a group of models.[[237]](#cite_note-240)

[![A blonde woman with a wet-looking hairstyle singing to a microphone on stage. She is wearing a black leather jacket.](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b6/GagaSpurs290722_%2833_of_39%29_%2852250248117%29_%28cropped_2%29.jpg/250px-GagaSpurs290722_%2833_of_39%29_%2852250248117%29_%28cropped_2%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:GagaSpurs290722_(33_of_39)_(52250248117)_(cropped_2).jpg)

Gaga performing on [The Chromatica Ball](/wiki/The_Chromatica_Ball "The Chromatica Ball"), her first all-stadium concert tour, in 2022[[238]](#cite_note-chromaticaballboxscore-241)

Having [endorsed](/wiki/List_of_Joe_Biden_2020_presidential_campaign_celebrity_endorsements "List of Joe Biden 2020 presidential campaign celebrity endorsements") Joe Biden in the [2020 United States presidential election](/wiki/2020_United_States_presidential_election "2020 United States presidential election"), Gaga sang the US national anthem during [his inauguration](/wiki/Inauguration_of_Joe_Biden "Inauguration of Joe Biden") as the country's 46th President on January 20, 2021.[[239]](#cite_note-242)[[240]](#cite_note-243) In February 2021, her dog walker Ryan Fischer was hospitalized after getting shot in Hollywood. Two of her [French Bulldogs](/wiki/French_Bulldog "French Bulldog"), Koji and Gustav, were taken while a third dog named Miss Asia escaped and was subsequently recovered by police. Gaga later offered a $500,000 reward for the return of her pets.[[241]](#cite_note-244)[[242]](#cite_note-245) Two days later, on February 26, a woman brought the dogs to a police station in Los Angeles. Both were unharmed. [Los Angeles Police](/wiki/Los_Angeles_Police "Los Angeles Police") initially said the woman who dropped off the dogs did not appear to be involved with the shooting,[[243]](#cite_note-246)[[244]](#cite_note-247) but on April 29, she was one of five people charged in connection with the shooting and theft.[[245]](#cite_note-248) In December 2022, James Howard Jackson, the man who shot Fischer, was sentenced to 21 years in prison.[[246]](#cite_note-249)

In April 2021, Gaga teamed up with Champagne brand [Dom Pérignon](/wiki/Dom_P%C3%A9rignon "Dom Pérignon"), and appeared in an ad shot by Nick Knight.[[247]](#cite_note-250) On September 3, she released her third remix album, *[Dawn of Chromatica](/wiki/Dawn_of_Chromatica "Dawn of Chromatica")*.[[248]](#cite_note-251) This was followed by her second collaborative album with Tony Bennett, titled *[Love for Sale](/wiki/Love_for_Sale_(Tony_Bennett_and_Lady_Gaga_album) "Love for Sale (Tony Bennett and Lady Gaga album)")*, on September 30.[[249]](#cite_note-252) The record received generally favorable reviews, and debuted at number eight in the US.[[250]](#cite_note-253)[[251]](#cite_note-254) The album's promotional rollout included the television special *[One Last Time: An Evening with Tony Bennett and Lady Gaga](/wiki/One_Last_Time:_An_Evening_with_Tony_Bennett_and_Lady_Gaga "One Last Time: An Evening with Tony Bennett and Lady Gaga")*, released in November 2021, on CBS, which featured select performances from the duo's August 3 and 5 performances at [Radio City Music Hall](/wiki/Radio_City_Music_Hall "Radio City Music Hall").[[252]](#cite_note-255)[[253]](#cite_note-VarietySpecials-256) Another taped performance by the duo recorded for *[MTV Unplugged](/wiki/MTV_Unplugged "MTV Unplugged")* was released that December.[[254]](#cite_note-EWunplugged-257) At the [64th Annual Grammy Awards](/wiki/64th_Annual_Grammy_Awards "64th Annual Grammy Awards"), *Love for Sale* won Gaga and Bennett the award for Best Traditional Pop Vocal Album.[[255]](#cite_note-258)

After an appearance in the television special *[Friends: The Reunion](/wiki/Friends:_The_Reunion "Friends: The Reunion")*, in which Gaga sang "[Smelly Cat](/wiki/Smelly_Cat "Smelly Cat")" with [Lisa Kudrow](/wiki/Lisa_Kudrow "Lisa Kudrow"),[[256]](#cite_note-259) she portrayed [Patrizia Reggiani](/wiki/Patrizia_Reggiani "Patrizia Reggiani"), who was convicted of hiring a hitman to murder her ex-husband and former head of the [Gucci](/wiki/Gucci "Gucci") fashion house [Maurizio Gucci](/wiki/Maurizio_Gucci "Maurizio Gucci") (played by [Adam Driver](/wiki/Adam_Driver "Adam Driver")), in [Ridley Scott](/wiki/Ridley_Scott "Ridley Scott")'s biographical crime film titled *[House of Gucci](/wiki/House_of_Gucci "House of Gucci")*.[[257]](#cite_note-260)[[258]](#cite_note-261) For the part, Gaga learned to speak with an Italian accent. She also stayed in character for 18 months, speaking with an accent for nine months during that period.[[259]](#cite_note-262) Her method acting took a toll on her mental wellbeing, and towards the end of filming she had to be accompanied on-set by a [psychiatric nurse](/wiki/Psychiatric_nurse "Psychiatric nurse").[[260]](#cite_note-263) The film was released on November 24, 2021, to mixed reviews, though critics praised Gaga's performance as "note-perfect".[[261]](#cite_note-264) She earned the [New York Film Critics Circle Award](/wiki/New_York_Film_Critics_Circle_Award_for_Best_Actress "New York Film Critics Circle Award for Best Actress"), and nominations for the [BAFTA Award](/wiki/75th_British_Academy_Film_Awards "75th British Academy Film Awards"), [Critics' Choice Award](/wiki/27th_Critics%27_Choice_Awards "27th Critics' Choice Awards"), [Golden Globe Award](/wiki/79th_Golden_Globe_Awards "79th Golden Globe Awards") and [Screen Actors Guild Award](/wiki/28th_Screen_Actors_Guild_Awards "28th Screen Actors Guild Awards") for Best Actress.[[262]](#cite_note-265)

Gaga co-wrote the song "[Hold My Hand](/wiki/Hold_My_Hand_(Lady_Gaga_song) "Hold My Hand (Lady Gaga song)")" for the 2022 film *[Top Gun: Maverick](/wiki/Top_Gun:_Maverick "Top Gun: Maverick")*,[[263]](#cite_note-266) and also composed [the score](/wiki/Top_Gun:_Maverick_(soundtrack) "Top Gun: Maverick (soundtrack)") alongside [Hans Zimmer](/wiki/Hans_Zimmer "Hans Zimmer") and [Harold Faltermeyer](/wiki/Harold_Faltermeyer "Harold Faltermeyer").[[264]](#cite_note-267) She performed "Hold My Hand" live at the [95th Academy Awards](/wiki/95th_Academy_Awards "95th Academy Awards"), where it was nominated for Best Original Song.[[265]](#cite_note-268) The track also earned Gaga her third win for Best Original Song at the [Satellite Awards](/wiki/27th_Satellite_Awards "27th Satellite Awards").[[266]](#cite_note-269) In July 2022, she embarked on [The Chromatica Ball](/wiki/The_Chromatica_Ball "The Chromatica Ball") stadium tour,[[267]](#cite_note-tour2022-270) which concluded that September.[[268]](#cite_note-miami-271) It grossed $112.4 million from 834,000 tickets sold throughout twenty dates and produced an HBO concert special titled *[Gaga Chromatica Ball](/wiki/Gaga_Chromatica_Ball "Gaga Chromatica Ball")*.[[238]](#cite_note-chromaticaballboxscore-241)[[269]](#cite_note-272) By the end of the year, she became the highest grossing female artist touring in 2022.[[270]](#cite_note-273) Gaga was appointed as co-chair of the [President's Committee on the Arts and Humanities](/wiki/President%27s_Committee_on_the_Arts_and_Humanities "President's Committee on the Arts and Humanities") by President Joe Biden in April 2023,[[271]](#cite_note-274) and collaborated with [the Rolling Stones](/wiki/The_Rolling_Stones "The Rolling Stones") on the song "[Sweet Sounds of Heaven](/wiki/Sweet_Sounds_of_Heaven "Sweet Sounds of Heaven")", also featuring [Stevie Wonder](/wiki/Stevie_Wonder "Stevie Wonder"), from their album *[Hackney Diamonds](/wiki/Hackney_Diamonds "Hackney Diamonds")* later that year.[[272]](#cite_note-275)

### 2024–present: *Joker: Folie à Deux* and *Mayhem*

[![A picture of a couple embracing each other. The woman standing on the left is wearing a black dress paired with a black headdress and a diamond necklace, and the man standing on the right is wearing a black tuxedo, a white shirt and a black bow tie.](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/ee/Lady_Gaga_and_Michael_Polansky-65154_%28cropped%29.jpg/250px-Lady_Gaga_and_Michael_Polansky-65154_%28cropped%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Lady_Gaga_and_Michael_Polansky-65154_(cropped).jpg)

Gaga with fiancé [Michael Polansky](/wiki/Michael_Polansky "Michael Polansky") (pictured in 2024), who co-wrote songs on *[Harlequin](/wiki/Harlequin_(Lady_Gaga_album) "Harlequin (Lady Gaga album)")* and *[Mayhem](/wiki/Mayhem_(Lady_Gaga_album) "Mayhem (Lady Gaga album)")* with her

Gaga was the featured artist for season two of the [online video game](/wiki/Online_video_game "Online video game") *[Fortnite](/wiki/Fortnite "Fortnite")* spin-off, *[Fortnite Festival](/wiki/Fortnite_Festival "Fortnite Festival")*, which ran from February to April 2024.[[273]](#cite_note-276) She became engaged to venture capitalist [Michael Polansky](/wiki/Michael_Polansky "Michael Polansky") that April, four years after they began dating.[[274]](#cite_note-Elle_2025-277)[[275]](#cite_note-vogue_2024-278) In July, she performed a rendition of [Zizi Jeanmaire](/wiki/Zizi_Jeanmaire "Zizi Jeanmaire")'s "Mon truc en plumes" at the [2024 Summer Olympics opening ceremony](/wiki/2024_Summer_Olympics_opening_ceremony "2024 Summer Olympics opening ceremony") in Paris.[[276]](#cite_note-279) Gaga released the single "[Die with a Smile](/wiki/Die_with_a_Smile "Die with a Smile")", a duet with [Bruno Mars](/wiki/Bruno_Mars "Bruno Mars"), on August 16, 2024.[[277]](#cite_note-280) In addition to topping the [*Billboard* Global 200](/wiki/Billboard_Global_200 "Billboard Global 200") for 18 weeks, it made Gaga the first act to achieve multiple US number-one songs in the 2000s, 2010s, and 2020s.[[278]](#cite_note-281)[[279]](#cite_note-282) The track was the most streamed song of 2025 globally by on-demand audio streams, with a total of 2.858 billion streams.[[280]](#cite_note-283) At the [67th Annual Grammy Awards](/wiki/67th_Annual_Grammy_Awards "67th Annual Grammy Awards"), it earned the award for Best Pop Duo/Group Performance, making Gaga the first artist to win the category three times.[[281]](#cite_note-284)

Gaga starred as [Harleen "Lee" Quinzel](/wiki/Harley_Quinn "Harley Quinn") alongside [Joaquin Phoenix](/wiki/Joaquin_Phoenix "Joaquin Phoenix") in [Todd Phillips](/wiki/Todd_Phillips "Todd Phillips")'s musical psychological thriller *[Joker: Folie à Deux](/wiki/Joker:_Folie_%C3%A0_Deux "Joker: Folie à Deux")*, the sequel to his 2019 film *[Joker](/wiki/Joker_(2019_film) "Joker (2019 film)")*.[[282]](#cite_note-285) It premiered at the [81st Venice International Film Festival](/wiki/81st_Venice_International_Film_Festival "81st Venice International Film Festival") and was released theatrically in October 2024.[[283]](#cite_note-286) While the film was a critical and commercial failure, Gaga's performance was better received, with critics finding her to be underused.[[284]](#cite_note-287)[[285]](#cite_note-288) Songs recorded by her and Phoenix for the film were included on an accompanying [soundtrack album](/wiki/Joker:_Folie_%C3%A0_Deux_(soundtrack) "Joker: Folie à Deux (soundtrack)").[[286]](#cite_note-289) Gaga additionally produced a companion album to the film, titled *[Harlequin](/wiki/Harlequin_(Lady_Gaga_album) "Harlequin (Lady Gaga album)")*, which was released on September 27, 2024.[[287]](#cite_note-290) She and Polansky co-wrote four of its tracks.[[288]](#cite_note-291) The two also created an associated concert film, *[Lady Gaga in Harlequin Live: One Night Only](/wiki/Lady_Gaga_in_Harlequin_Live:_One_Night_Only "Lady Gaga in Harlequin Live: One Night Only")*, released on December 24, 2025.[[289]](#cite_note-292)

Gaga's album titled *[Mayhem](/wiki/Mayhem_(Lady_Gaga_album) "Mayhem (Lady Gaga album)")*—conceived as a pop record per Polansky's recommendation—was released on March 7, 2025, to critical acclaim and debuted at number one in the US.[[275]](#cite_note-vogue_2024-278)[[290]](#cite_note-293)[[291]](#cite_note-294) The couple served as executive producers for the album,[[292]](#cite_note-295) and wrote seven tracks together.[[274]](#cite_note-Elle_2025-277) This included the lead single "[Disease](/wiki/Disease_(Lady_Gaga_song) "Disease (Lady Gaga song)")", which was released on October 25, 2024,[[293]](#cite_note-296) and peaked at number seven in the UK.[[294]](#cite_note-297) Its second single, "[Abracadabra](/wiki/Abracadabra_(Lady_Gaga_song) "Abracadabra (Lady Gaga song)")", followed on February 3, 2025,[[295]](#cite_note-298) reaching the top five in Germany and the UK, as well as on the Global 200 chart.[[296]](#cite_note-299)[[297]](#cite_note-300)[[298]](#cite_note-301) The track list also included "Die with a Smile" as a later addition.[[299]](#cite_note-302) Gaga sang "Hold My Hand" at the [Super Bowl LIX](/wiki/Super_Bowl_LIX "Super Bowl LIX") pregame as a tribute to the victims of the [2025 New Orleans truck attack](/wiki/2025_New_Orleans_truck_attack "2025 New Orleans truck attack"), which won her the award for Outstanding Music Direction at the [46th Sports Emmy Awards](/wiki/46th_Sports_Emmy_Awards "46th Sports Emmy Awards").[[300]](#cite_note-sports_emmy-303) In March 2025, she hosted *Saturday Night Live* for a second time.[[301]](#cite_note-304) At the [2025 iHeartRadio Music Awards](/wiki/2025_iHeartRadio_Music_Awards "2025 iHeartRadio Music Awards"), she was honored with the Innovator Award.[[302]](#cite_note-305)

Gaga performed a [series of promotional concerts](/wiki/Mayhem_promotional_concerts "Mayhem promotional concerts") in support of *Mayhem* between April and May 2025, before embarking on [The Mayhem Ball](/wiki/The_Mayhem_Ball "The Mayhem Ball") concert tour the following July.[[303]](#cite_note-306) The former included a free concert at [Copacabana Beach](/wiki/Copacabana_Beach "Copacabana Beach"), Rio de Janeiro, as part of the "[Todo Mundo no Rio](/wiki/Todo_Mundo_no_Rio "Todo Mundo no Rio")" initiative,[[304]](#cite_note-307) which became the [most-attended concert by a female artist](/wiki/List_of_most-attended_concerts "List of most-attended concerts") with an estimated audience of around 2.1 to 2.5 million.[[d]](#cite_note-311) In September 2025, Gaga appeared in season two of the Netflix series *[Wednesday](/wiki/Wednesday_(TV_series) "Wednesday (TV series)")*, portraying the ghost of a former Nevermore Academy teacher Rosaline Rotwood.[[308]](#cite_note-312) She also released a song titled "[The Dead Dance](/wiki/The_Dead_Dance "The Dead Dance")", accompanied by a [Tim Burton](/wiki/Tim_Burton "Tim Burton")-directed music video, which was featured in the series.[[309]](#cite_note-313)

At the [68th Annual Grammy Awards](/wiki/68th_Annual_Grammy_Awards "68th Annual Grammy Awards"), Gaga won Best Pop Vocal Album (*Mayhem*) and [Best Dance Pop Recording](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Best_Dance_Pop_Recording "Grammy Award for Best Dance Pop Recording") ("Abracadabra").[[310]](#cite_note-314) During [Bad Bunny](/wiki/Bad_Bunny "Bad Bunny")'s [Super Bowl LX halftime show](/wiki/Super_Bowl_LX_halftime_show "Super Bowl LX halftime show") one week later, she made a guest appearance and performed a [salsa](/wiki/Salsa_music "Salsa music") version of "Die with a Smile", accompanied by the group Los Sobrinos.[[311]](#cite_note-315)

## Artistry

### Influences

[![A woman with plaited hair, blue eyes and red lipstick wearing a colorful dress and guitar strap.](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b1/Madonna_Rebel_Heart_Tour_2015_-_Stockholm_%2823051472299%29_%28cropped%29.jpg/250px-Madonna_Rebel_Heart_Tour_2015_-_Stockholm_%2823051472299%29_%28cropped%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Madonna_Rebel_Heart_Tour_2015_-_Stockholm_(23051472299)_(cropped).jpg)

[![A man smiling; he has brown hair and wears a suit jacket and vest, and a white shirt open at the collar. His blue tie is not fastened.](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d6/David_Bowie_%281987%29.jpg/250px-David_Bowie_%281987%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:David_Bowie_(1987).jpg)

Musicians such as [Madonna](/wiki/Madonna "Madonna") and [David Bowie](/wiki/David_Bowie "David Bowie") have influenced Gaga.

Gaga grew up listening to artists such as Michael Jackson,[[312]](#cite_note-316) [the Beatles](/wiki/The_Beatles "The Beatles"), Stevie Wonder, Queen, [Bruce Springsteen](/wiki/Bruce_Springsteen "Bruce Springsteen"), [Pink Floyd](/wiki/Pink_Floyd "Pink Floyd"), [Led Zeppelin](/wiki/Led_Zeppelin "Led Zeppelin"), [Whitney Houston](/wiki/Whitney_Houston "Whitney Houston"),[[313]](#cite_note-She's_The_Greatest_Of_All_Time-317) Elton John, [Prince](/wiki/Prince_(musician) "Prince (musician)"),[[314]](#cite_note-318)[[315]](#cite_note-319) [En Vogue](/wiki/En_Vogue "En Vogue"), [TLC](/wiki/TLC_(group) "TLC (group)"),[[316]](#cite_note-320) Christina Aguilera,[[317]](#cite_note-321) [Janet Jackson](/wiki/Janet_Jackson "Janet Jackson"),[[318]](#cite_note-322) and [Blondie](/wiki/Blondie_(band) "Blondie (band)"),[[319]](#cite_note-dharry-323) who have all influenced her music.[[320]](#cite_note-324) Gaga's musical inspiration varies from [dance-pop](/wiki/Dance-pop "Dance-pop") singers such as [Madonna](/wiki/Madonna "Madonna") and Michael Jackson to glam rock artists such as David Bowie and [Freddie Mercury](/wiki/Freddie_Mercury "Freddie Mercury"), as well as the theatrics of the [pop artist](/wiki/Pop_art "Pop art") Andy Warhol and her own performance roots in musical theater.[[34]](#cite_note-Allmusic_bio-35)[[321]](#cite_note-325) She has been compared to Madonna, who has said that she sees herself reflected in Gaga,[[322]](#cite_note-326) while Gaga has expressed her desire to revolutionize pop music the way Madonna did.[[323]](#cite_note-327) Gaga has also cited heavy metal bands as an influence, specifically [Iron Maiden](/wiki/Iron_Maiden "Iron Maiden"), [Black Sabbath](/wiki/Black_Sabbath "Black Sabbath"), [Nine Inch Nails](/wiki/Nine_Inch_Nails "Nine Inch Nails") and [Marilyn Manson](/wiki/Marilyn_Manson_(band) "Marilyn Manson (band)").[[324]](#cite_note-328) She has credited Beyoncé as a key inspiration to pursue a musical career.[[325]](#cite_note-329) Her other inspirations include [Grace Jones](/wiki/Grace_Jones "Grace Jones"), [Duke Ellington](/wiki/Duke_Ellington "Duke Ellington"), and [Carole King](/wiki/Carole_King "Carole King").[[326]](#cite_note-330)

Gaga was inspired by her mother to be interested in fashion, which would later become a major influence and integrated with her music.[[20]](#cite_note-mtv_55-21)[[327]](#cite_note-timesonline2-331) Stylistically, Gaga has been compared to [Leigh Bowery](/wiki/Leigh_Bowery "Leigh Bowery"), [Isabella Blow](/wiki/Isabella_Blow "Isabella Blow"), and [Cher](/wiki/Cher "Cher");[[328]](#cite_note-ourladyvogue-332)[[329]](#cite_note-cherfashionsense-333) she once commented that as a child, she absorbed Cher's fashion sense and made it her own.[[329]](#cite_note-cherfashionsense-333) Gaga became friends with British fashion designer [Alexander McQueen](/wiki/Alexander_McQueen "Alexander McQueen") shortly before his suicide in 2010, and became known for wearing his designs, particularly his towering [armadillo shoes](/wiki/Armadillo_shoe "Armadillo shoe").[[98]](#cite_note-comeparty-100)[[330]](#cite_note-334) She has called fashion designer [Donatella Versace](/wiki/Donatella_Versace "Donatella Versace") her [muse](/wiki/Muse "Muse"); Versace referred to Gaga as "the fresh Donatella".[[331]](#cite_note-Times_Online.co.uk-335)[[332]](#cite_note-336) Gaga has also been influenced by [Princess Diana](/wiki/Diana,_Princess_of_Wales "Diana, Princess of Wales"), whom she has admired since childhood.[[333]](#cite_note-337)

Gaga has called the Indian [alternative medicine](/wiki/Alternative_medicine "Alternative medicine") advocate [Deepak Chopra](/wiki/Deepak_Chopra "Deepak Chopra") a "true inspiration",[[334]](#cite_note-338) and has also quoted Indian leader [Osho](/wiki/Rajneesh "Rajneesh")'s book *Creativity* on Twitter. Gaga said she was influenced by Osho's work in valuing rebellion through creativity and equality.[[335]](#cite_note-339)

### Musical style and themes

Critics have analyzed and scrutinized Gaga's musical and performance style, as she has experimented with new ideas and images throughout her career.
